title: Matched-lead record (v5.4.0 pipeline stage 4 output — one record in matched_leads.json)
description: A record in matched_leads.json, the idempotent aggregator output (19_aggregator_idempotency_rule.md). Each matched lead carries one or more aggregated signals; each signal is a group of leads-base records that shared the full 18.B aggregation key, merged per the 18.C within-group contract and the 18.D cross-source rule. matched_leads.json is the aggregate output and is NEVER an aggregator input (19.C). Every matched lead MUST carry at least one signal from a PRIMARY_EVENT_SOURCE (13.5 row provenance rule). v5.4.0 Session 7A (see 17.K) adds the multi-owner block, carried from the matched lead's representative leads-base record; owner_name stays the required primary display owner and old single-owner records remain valid.

lead_id.description: Unique id for the matched lead. Deterministic given the same base-file inputs (19.D idempotency invariant).

primary_parcel_id.description: Resolved county-canonical parcel id, or null when parcel_resolution_status is UNRESOLVED / REVIEW_REQUIRED (13.14).

owner_name.description: The debtor / lead subject per 17 — the PRIMARY display owner. The 17.E placeholder when parcel_resolution_status is REVIEW_REQUIRED. Never a filer (20 Check 12). Backward-compatible: required, unchanged by Session 7A.

filer_entity.description: Filer name (17.E). A known-filer pattern may appear here and only here, never in owner_name.

review_reason.description: 17.E routing reason. Non-null when parcel_resolution_status is REVIEW_REQUIRED.

parcel_resolution_status.description: 13.14.1 parcel resolution status. Session 7A: the downstream needs-review verdict field; multi_owner_status never carries a review verdict.

enrichment_status.description: 13.14.1 enrichment status, tracked independently of parcel_resolution_status.

signals.description: Aggregated signals on this lead. minItems 1: a lead with zero signals is not a lead (13.5). Each item is one 18.B aggregation group.

aggregation_key.description: The 18.B key tuple this group collapsed on.

signal_type.description: Operator-facing display label (18.B). The signal chip text; the count badge shows count when count > 1.

count.description: Number of base records in this group (18.C). count > 1 is legitimate stacking only when it equals the number of distinct instrument_numbers (18.E); count > distinct instrument count is a dedup failure.

instrument_numbers.description: Distinct instrument numbers across the group (18.C). The 18.E union key.

source_urls.description: Distinct source proof URLs across the group (18.C / 18.D).

evidence_ids.description: Distinct evidence ledger entry ids across the group (18.C).

source_ids.description: Distinct source_ids contributing to this group. More than one entry means a 18.D cross-source merge.
Distinct source_ids contributing any signal to this lead.

evidence_ids.description: Union of all evidence ledger entry ids across this lead's signals.

lead_status.description: Lead lifecycle status per FRAMEWORK_VERSION.json lead_status_lifecycle / 09_output_schemas.md. Optional at aggregator output; the dashboard-build stage sets it.

owners.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A multi-owner block — every distressed owner identified, carried from the matched lead's representative leads-base record. Co-owners are NEVER dropped. A single-owner record carries exactly one entry, is_primary true. Optional for backward compatibility; old single-owner records that omit the multi-owner block remain valid.

primary_owner_name.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — the primary display owner; equals owner_name when multi_owner_status is SINGLE_OWNER or MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_CLEAR; the 17.E placeholder when MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR.

additional_owner_names.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — names of every non-primary owner. Empty for SINGLE_OWNER.

owner_count.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — count of entries in owners.

multi_owner_status.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — DESCRIPTIVE owner-cardinality status, NOT a review verdict. The needs-review verdict on a matched lead is parcel_resolution_status (= REVIEW_REQUIRED), the downstream F-1 propagation. multi_owner_status has no REVIEW_REQUIRED value; when the primary owner is unclear it is MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR and parcel_resolution_status is independently REVIEW_REQUIRED — the descriptive and verdict fields never contradict.

allOf.description: v5.4.0 Session 7A — SINGLE_OWNER: exactly one owner, marked primary; owner_count 1; no additional owners.
v5.4.0 Session 7A — MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_CLEAR: more than one owner, exactly one marked primary; owner_count > 1.

v5.4.0 Session 7A — MULTIPLE_OWNERS_PRIMARY_UNCLEAR: more than one owner, NONE marked primary; owner_count > 1. multi_owner_status only describes; the verdict is separate — parcel_resolution_status MUST independently be REVIEW_REQUIRED, so the descriptive and verdict fields never contradict.
